note: False-positive rate under placebo timing should stay low.
Placebo t-statistics should not mimic real treatment effects.
Observed DID coefficient should not look extreme under random treatment shuffles.
Direction should remain stable under leave-one-continent-out splits.
Cross-continent DID dispersion should remain bounded.
At least a minimal subset of design tracks should pass pretrend geometry diagnostics.
Few-shock fallback used: sign consistency is more stable than coefficient of variation with only 2-3 shocks.
Fallback check used: no explicit placebo-year rows available in export.
Multiple-testing table should contain non-trivial hypothesis families.
Post-correction signal share should remain non-zero.
